user_message: Hey, can you draft a response for Fabrikam Residences?

input_artifacts.content:
From: Kevin Lee <kevin.lee@fabrikamresi.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-24 10:06 EST
Subject: URGENT: territory planning Issue at Fabrikam Residences

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about territory planning. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Kevin Lee <kevin.lee@fabrikamresi.com>
Date: 2024-02-16 12:16 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Fabrikam Residences territory planning

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about territory planning. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Kevin Lee <kevin.lee@fabrikamresi.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-28 13:45 EST
Subject: territory planning - Action Required

Our team encountered some challenges with territory planning. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

Meeting: territory planning Discussion - Fabrikam Residences
Date: 2024-02-07
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft), Kevin (Fabrikam Residences)

[00:01:30] Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into territory planning.

[00:07:00] Kevin (Fabrikam Residences): Let me add some context -- we've been working on budget approval and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

[00:12:00] Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is timeline constraints.

[00:15:00] Kevin (Fabrikam Residences): The timeline for partner engagement is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:18:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is cost optimization.

[00:21:00] Kevin (Fabrikam Residences): The timeline for stakeholder alignment is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:24:00] Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about budget approval. Can we address those?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-L trap (Draft-only posture delays time-sensitive sends); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-L failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-L failure mode (Draft-only posture delays time-sensitive sends)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct territory planning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
